identity.name: Product Assignment
identity.definition: A Product Assignment is a scoped provisioning record that declares a Solution or Product available to a defined population within a school or district.

representations.heading: Card layout
representations.description: How a Product Assignment appears in provisioning dashboards

attributes.name: Scope Level
attributes.description: Granularity of assignment: school, grade, or class.

attributes.name: Scope Value
attributes.description: The specific school, grade, or class this assignment targets.

representations.heading: List layout
representations.description: How a Product Assignment appears in assignment management tables

attributes.name: Scope Level
attributes.description: Granularity of assignment: school, grade, or class.

attributes.name: Scope Value
attributes.description: The specific school, grade, or class this assignment targets.

representations.heading: Detail page layout
representations.description: What users see when they open a full Product Assignment record

attributes.name: Assignment ID
attributes.description: Unique system identifier for this assignment.

attributes.name: Scope Level
attributes.description: Granularity of assignment: school, grade, or class.

attributes.name: Scope Value
attributes.description: The specific school, grade, or class this assignment targets.

attributes.name: Product ID
attributes.description: Individual Product being assigned (if not via Solution).

attributes.name: Effective Date
attributes.description: When this assignment takes effect. Can be future-dated.

attributes.name: School ID
attributes.description: The school this assignment applies to.

stories.title: Assign Solution to School
stories.benefit: all teachers and students at that school gain access to the included Products.
stories.whenClause: I assign a Solution to a school,
stories.thenClause: a Product Assignment record is created and all users at the school can access the Solution's products.

stories.title: Review Assignment Coverage
stories.benefit: I can verify which schools, grades, or classes have access to which Solutions.
stories.whenClause: I view the Product Assignment list,
stories.thenClause: I see all assignments with their scope, Solution, state, and creation details.

stories.title: Rollover Assignments
stories.benefit: schools retain access to their Solutions without manual re-creation each year.
stories.thenClause: active assignments are duplicated for the new school year with updated effective dates.

businessRules.title: Uniqueness
businessRules.description: A Product Assignment is uniquely identified by its Assignment ID. A given scope (school + grade + class) can have only one active assignment per Solution.

businessRules.title: Scope Hierarchy
businessRules.description: School-level assignments cascade to all grades and classes within. Grade-level assignments override school-level for that grade. Class-level is the most specific.

businessRules.title: Solution or Product
businessRules.description: An assignment references either a Solution ID or a Product ID, but not both. Solution assignments implicitly grant access to all Products within.

businessRules.title: Effective Dating
businessRules.description: Assignments with a future effective date are visible but not active until that date. Past effective dates activate immediately.

businessRules.title: Deactivation
businessRules.description: Deactivating an assignment immediately revokes access for the target population. Historical data from usage during the active period is retained.

businessRules.title: Deletion
businessRules.description: Only inactive assignments can be deleted. Active assignments must be deactivated first. Deletion removes the record permanently.

businessRules.title: Audit Trail
businessRules.description: All assignment changes (creation, edits, deactivation, reactivation, deletion) are logged with the acting admin's identity and timestamp.

states.name: Active
states.description: Assignment is in effect and granting access to the target population
states.triggers: Admin creates assignment or reactivates an inactive one

states.name: Inactive
states.description: Assignment is suspended; access revoked but record retained

relationships.mechanics: A Product Assignment references a Solution to grant access to all of its included Products. The admin selects a Solution during assignment creation.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-one. Many Product Assignments can reference the same Solution; each assignment references at most one Solution.
relationships.sorts: Assignments for a Solution: by scope level, by school name, by creation date.
relationships.filters: By state (active/inactive), by scope level, by effective date range.
relationships.dependencies: If a Solution is deprecated, existing active assignments remain in effect but no new assignments can be created.

relationships.mechanics: A Product Assignment targets a School (directly or via the scope value). The school is the organizational unit that receives access.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-one. A School can have many Product Assignments; each assignment belongs to one School.
relationships.sorts: Assignments for a School: by Solution name, by scope level, by state.
relationships.filters: By state, by Solution, by effective date.
relationships.dependencies: If a School is deactivated, its Product Assignments are automatically deactivated.

nestedObjects.name: Solution
nestedObjects.description: The Solution being provisioned by this assignment

allAttributes.name: Assignment ID
allAttributes.description: Unique system identifier for this Product Assignment.

allAttributes.name: Scope Level
allAttributes.description: Granularity of assignment: school, grade, or class.

allAttributes.name: Scope Value
allAttributes.description: The specific school, grade, or class this assignment targets.

allAttributes.name: Solution ID
allAttributes.description: The Solution being assigned. Mutually exclusive with Product ID.

allAttributes.name: Product ID
allAttributes.description: Individual Product being assigned. Mutually exclusive with Solution ID.

allAttributes.name: State
allAttributes.description: Active or inactive. Controls whether the assignment grants access.

allAttributes.name: Created By
allAttributes.description: Identity of the admin who created this assignment.

allAttributes.name: Created At
allAttributes.description: Timestamp of when this assignment was created.

allAttributes.name: Effective Date
allAttributes.description: When this assignment takes effect. Defaults to creation date if not specified.

allAttributes.name: School ID
allAttributes.description: The school this assignment applies to.

structure.evidence: A Product Assignment has rich attributes: scope level, scope value, Solution/Product reference, state, creation metadata, effective date, and school reference. A detail page can be designed.

instances.evidence: Lincoln Elementary → Star 360 (school-wide), 3rd Grade → Freckle + Lalilo (grade-level), Mrs. Smith's class → myON (class-level). Every district has dozens of assignment instances.

purpose.evidence: Product Assignments are the mechanism by which schools and districts gain access to Renaissance products. Admins create, manage, and audit assignments to control provisioning.

sipValidation.verdict: Domain object. The provisioning bridge between Solutions/Products and the organizational hierarchy.

synonyms.notes: Technical term for the entitlement record
Used when discussing seat counts and licensing
Used when diagnosing why a user can or cannot access a product

shapeshifterMatrix.description: Product assignments shown as cards with scope, solution, and status. Admins manage which products are provisioned to which schools or districts.
Product assignments listed in a compact table with state and ownership. Admins activate, deactivate, or review provisioning records.
The complete product assignment record — scope, solution, effective date, and all management controls. Admins configure and adjust provisioning from this page.
Product assignments shown as compact rows within a school's overview, listing which solutions are active at that school.
